item.title: What to do when the ‘public good’ of information goes bad
item.description: The creation and dissemination of reliable news is at an economic disadvantage

item.title: America’s gig economy
item.description: Self-employment, at both the high and the low end, is keeping consumption afloat — but for how long?

item.title: We said we didn’t want you working from home. Well it turns out we do
item.description: A behind-the-scenes look at the work of Rutherford Hall, critical communications strategist

item.title: Should I change the name of my house?
item.description: The right one can add romance or gravitas, and tell a story — the wrong one can be naff, and scupper a sale

item.title: Investors need to rethink the ‘factor’ approach
item.description: Portfolio management should be based more on themes such as innovation

item.title: The Iran war will cement China’s superpower status
item.description: Beijing’s industrial prowess positions it for economic and diplomatic gains

item.title: Social media has been put on notice
item.description: Legal and legislative pressure is mounting to safeguard young people

item.title: Iran’s aggression will bring Gulf countries closer to the US
item.description: Despite their resentment, states in the region need American and Israeli technology to defend themselves

item.title: The Pentagon-Anthropic dispute is a test of control
item.description: Should private companies be able to set boundaries around the AI systems we integrate into our lives?

item.title: The hunger for ‘content’ is keeping us culturally stuck
item.description: The algorithms of the distraction economy are feeding us what we already like, creating a loop with no progress

item.title: In a world of $100 oil, fast fashion loses its defensive charms
item.description: Polyester-heavy retailers could soon start to feel the cost of their exposure to higher oil prices

item.title: The surprising science of quitting
item.description: Most of us are just one event away from resigning even though we probably should stay put

item.title: The failures of prediction markets are predictable
item.description: They can send wrong signals when trading is illiquid or when herd behaviour takes hold

item.title: Chart of the Week: The oil shock hurts poorer economies hardest
item.description: Developing economies are more dependent on energy

item.title: UK stocks are handling 2026 much better than the UK economy
item.description: The UK relies on energy imports, which are surging in price, but many FTSE 100 companies have benefited from the volatility

item.title: No BS: using corporate jargon is really giving you away
item.description: A new study suggests that buying into the blather reveals a credulous worker

item.title: Here’s the oil shock that Donald Trump can’t control
item.description: While there is a lot of spare crude on ships and in storage, stocks of refined products are much smaller

item.title: Social media is populist and polarising; AI may be the opposite
item.description: Large language models elevate expert consensus and moderate views, in sharp contrast to social platforms

item.title: Cash is still king this bonus season (can somebody tell the chancellor?)
item.description: Volatile markets show the vital role cash plays in investment Isa portfolios

item.title: The wearing question of what to wear
item.description: Theme parties are more popular than ever — have you got something ‘banging’ on?

item.title: Would you like some intrusive curiosity with that?
item.description: Waiters who ask about my day may mean well. That doesn’t mean I have to answer them

item.title: Aces Foodcraft, London: Engrossing, exciting and unashamedly inventive — restaurant review
item.description: Alex Craciun’s Japanese-inspired Fitzrovia restaurant has some weird ideas — and pulls them off brilliantly

item.title: Are you suffering from ‘subscription fatigue’?
item.description: Sick of not owning my music collection any more, I’ve returned to vinyl with gusto — and, to my surprise, Gen Z is with me

item.title: All hail Sassicaia, king of the Supertuscans
item.description: Plus, Jancis Robinson’s advice on storing half-drunk bottles

item.title: The Trump coin and lessons from the Ostrogoths
item.description: The use of his image on a gold offering from the US Mint is a sign of how little power he actually has over America’s money
